verses.commentary: Famine still severe. Pressure continues.
When grain eaten. Buy more food. Supplies exhausted. Jacob initiates.
Judah speaks (not Reuben).
Conditional offer. Benjamin is key.
We won't go. Firm position. Brothers united.
Blames them for revealing Benjamin's existence.
The brothers defend themselves. How could we know? But they're learning: in God's plan, every detail matters.
Urgent appeal. Survival at stake.
Hold me responsible. Stronger than Reuben's (42:37). No conditions.
Time wasted. Urgency emphasized. Delay is costly.
Take best products. As gift. Jacob relents. Products of Canaan. Like Jacob sending gifts to Esau (32:13-21).
Take double silver. Perhaps mistake. Honest dealing.
Take your brother. Go at once. Benjamin will go.
May God Almighty (El Shaddai, אֵל שַׁדַּי) grant mercy. Jacob prays. Invokes covenant God. Like Esther: if I perish, I perish (Esther 4:16).
Double silver. And Benjamin. Hurried to Egypt. Obedience. Full compliance. Journey begins.
Slaughter animal. Prepare meal. Special treatment. Meal invitation. Honor.
The man did as Joseph told him and took the men to Joseph's house. Compliance.
Men frightened. Overpower. Seize as slaves. Take donkeys. Fear dominates. They expect trap.
Spoke at entrance.
Deferential. Humble approach.
Opened sacks. Brought it back. Honest recounting. They didn't steal.
Extra payment. Claim ignorance. Complete honesty.

Your God, the God of your father, has given you treasure in your sacks; I received your silver. It's all right. Don't be afraid. Your God, God of your father, gave treasure. Brought Simeon out. Attributes silver to divine gift. He received their payment (Joseph's instruction). Simeon released. Relief.
Fodder for donkeys. Hospitality customs. Honored guests. Like Abraham (18:4).
Prepared gifts. Gift ready. Meal anticipated.
Second fulfillment of dreams (37:7, 9).
Joseph inquires about Jacob after seventeen years of separation. The question carries longing beneath its formality.
Good news. Jacob lives. They bow again.
His mother's son. Benjamin is Rachel's child, the only other one. God be gracious to you, my son. The blessing nearly breaks his composure.
Hurried out. Wept there. Sees Benjamin grown. Private weeping. Second time (42:24).
Washed face. Controlling himself. Serve food. Composes himself. Returns. Maintains facade. Meal begins.
Segregated dining. Cultural separation. Egyptian prejudice against shepherds (46:34).
They're seated by birth order. How could he know? Supernatural knowledge? The mystery unnerves them.
Benjamin gets five times more. Testing the brothers: will they resent favoritism toward Rachel's son? Or have they changed since selling Joseph? They feast together.
